2605.04729.title: AISSA: Implementation and Deployment of an AI-based Student Slides Analysis tool for Academic Presentations
2605.04729.paper_summary: Providing timely and actionable feedback on oral presentation slides is challenging in higher education, particularly in large classes where teachers cannot realistically deliver detailed formative feedback before students present. This paper introduces AISSA (AI-based Student Slides Analysis tool), a web-based system that combines large language models (LLMs) and Learning Analytics dashboards to support scalable, rubric-based feedback on presentation slides. AISSA allows students to upload their slide decks prior to an oral presentation and automatically receive quantitative scores and qualitative feedback based on teacher-defined evaluation rubrics. The system analyzes both slide-level features and slide content, generates structured feedback through an LLM (ChatGPT 5.2), and presents the results through interactive dashboards for students and teachers. We tested AISSA on a pilot deployment with 46 undergraduate students in a real academic setting. The results indicate that AISSA is technically reliable, economically feasible, and perceived by students as useful for iterative slide improvement. These findings suggest that combining LLM-based analysis with Learning Analytics dashboards is a promising approach for supporting formative feedback on presentation slides at scale.
2605.04729.paper_summary_zh: 提供及時且可行的口頭報告幻燈片反饋在高等教育中是具有挑戰性的，特別是在大型課堂中，教師無法在學生報告前實際提供詳細的形成性反饋。本文介紹了AISSA（基於AI的學生幻燈片分析工具），這是一個基於網絡的系統，結合了大型語言模型（LLMs）和學習分析儀表板，以支持可擴展的、基於評分標準的幻燈片反饋。AISSA允許學生在口頭報告前上傳他們的幻燈片，並根據教師定義的評估標準自動接收定量分數和定性反饋。該系統分析幻燈片層級特徵和幻燈片內容，通過LLM（ChatGPT 5.2）生成結構化反饋，並通過互動儀表板向學生和教師展示結果。我們在一個真實的學術環境中對46名本科生進行了AISSA的試點部署測試。結果表明，AISSA在技術上可靠，經濟上可行，並且被學生認為對於迭代幻燈片改進是有用的。這些發現表明，將基於LLM的分析與學習分析儀表板結合是一種有前景的方法，可以在大規模上支持口頭報告幻燈片的形成性反饋。
2605.04729.author: Alvaro Becerra et.al.
2605.04729.authors: Alvaro Becerra, Diego Gomez, Ruth Cobos
